title: xyris bracteata
intro: Xyris bracteata, also known as the yellow-eyed grass, is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Xyris bracteata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Xyris bracteata are typically made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
